ucs.title: [LLM] Naikon (PLA Unit 78020) Aria-body / RainyDay DLL search-order hijack via signed-binary side-load
ucs.description: Hunts Naikon's signature delivery technique: dropping a legitimately-signed Microsoft/AV/VPN executable into AppData, Temp, Public or ProgramData and using DLL search-order hijacking so the executable side-loads a malicious DLL (Aria-body, RainyDay, Nebulae) from the same folder. CrowdStrike tracks Naikon as part of the Lotus Panda/Override Panda activity cluster; Kaspersky and Bitdefender both documented this exact loader pattern across SE-Asian government and military intrusions (2015-2021).
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as parent_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_path IN ("*\\AppData\\*","*\\Temp\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*") AND (Processes.process_company="Microsoft Corporation" OR Processes.process_company="Kaspersky*" OR Processes.process_company="ESET*" OR Processes.process_company="Symantec*" OR Processes.process_company="VMware*" OR Processes.process_company="Adobe*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_hash
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| join type=inner host process_path [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" AND NOT Filesystem.file_path IN ("C:\\Windows\\*","C:\\Program Files*\\*") by host Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_hash
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
    | rex field=file_path "(?<dll_dir>.*)\\\\[^\\\\]+$"
    | rename dll_dir as process_path file_hash as dll_hash file_name as dll_name
]
| where dll_hash != process_hash
| table firstSeen host user process_name process_path process_hash dll_name dll_hash cmdline
ucs.kql:
// Naikon Aria-body / RainyDay side-load: signed exe in user-writeable path
// loads a non-system DLL from the SAME folder.
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let _user_writeable = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\"]);
let _signed_companies = dynamic(["Microsoft Corporation","Kaspersky Lab","ESET","ESET, spol. s r.o.","Symantec Corporation","VMware, Inc.","Adobe Inc.","Adobe Systems Incorporated","Avast Software","Outlook"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_user_writeable)              // dropped to a writeable path — Naikon TTP
| where InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName in~ (_signed_companies)    // legit signed loader binary
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\")                         // DLL not from system
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256                                  // distinct file (not the exe itself)
| extend ExeDir = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
| extend DllDir = tolower(FolderPath)
| where DllDir == ExeDir                                                   // DLL planted next to the exe
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          LoaderExe = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          LoaderPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          LoaderSigner = InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
          LoaderSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          SideLoadedDll = FileName,
          SideLoadedSHA256 = SHA256,
          InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Requires Sysmon EID 7 (Image Loaded) ingested via WindowsEvent.
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let _writeable_rx = @"(?i)\\(AppData|Temp|Public|ProgramData)\\";
let _signed = dynamic(["Microsoft Corporation","Kaspersky Lab","ESET","Symantec Corporation","VMware, Inc.","Adobe Inc.","Avast Software"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
| extend Image = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         LoaderCompany = tostring(EventData.Company),
         LoaderSigned = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         LoadedHash = tostring(EventData.Hashes),
         User = tostring(EventData.User)
| where Image matches regex _writeable_rx
| where LoaderCompany in~ (_signed)
| where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
| where not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Windows\") and not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| extend ExeDir = tolower(strcat_array(array_slice(split(Image, @"\"), 0, -2), @"\")),
         DllDir = tolower(strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLoaded, @"\"), 0, -2), @"\"))
| where ExeDir == DllDir
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User, LoaderExe = Image, LoaderCompany, LoaderSigned,
          SideLoadedDll = ImageLoaded, LoadedHash
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Naikon Aria-body / RainyDay Signed-Binary DLL Side-Load
id: a3d7f8b2-9c11-4e8f-9b6a-7c4d8e2a1f30
status: experimental
description: Detects legitimately-signed loader binaries (Microsoft, Kaspersky, ESET, Symantec, VMware, Adobe) executing from user-writeable paths and loading a non-system DLL from the same directory. This is the canonical Naikon (Lotus Panda, PLA Unit 78020) Aria-body / RainyDay / Nebulae loader pattern.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0019/
  - https://securelist.com/the-naikon-apt/
  - https://www.bitdefender.com/files/News/CaseStudies/study/388/Bitdefender-PR-Whitepaper-NAIKON-creat5397-en-EN.pdf
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.t1574.001
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.defense_evasion
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: image_load
detection:
  selection_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
  selection_signed:
    Company:
      - 'Microsoft Corporation'
      - 'Kaspersky Lab'
      - 'ESET'
      - 'Symantec Corporation'
      - 'VMware, Inc.'
      - 'Adobe Inc.'
      - 'Avast Software'
  selection_dll:
    ImageLoaded|endswith: '.dll'
  filter_system:
    ImageLoaded|startswith:
      - 'C:\Windows\'
      - 'C:\Program Files'
  condition: all of selection_* and not filter_system
fields:
  - Image
  - ImageLoaded
  - Company
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Portable installers that legitimately ship signed binaries with bundled DLLs
  - Software updaters staging files in ProgramData
level: high
ucs.rationale: Naikon's defining tradecraft across every public report (Kaspersky 2015 'CameraShy', Bitdefender 2020 Aria-body, ESET 2022 Nebulae) is signed-binary DLL side-loading from user-writeable paths. Generic side-load detections fire on installers; pinning the parent's signer to a small list of impersonated vendors AND requiring the DLL to sit next to the exe is what makes this Naikon-specific rather tha

_field_issues.message: `InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName` is not a column of DeviceImageLoadEvents; it does exist on DeviceProcessEvents, DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceFileEvents.

ucs.title: [LLM] Naikon WMIC reconnaissance burst followed by scheduled-task or WMI persistence within 30 min
ucs.description: Naikon operators repeatedly run a stereotyped WMIC discovery sequence (process list, antivirus product enumeration, OS/computersystem, NIC config, domain accounts) immediately before installing persistence via schtasks.exe or 'wmic process call create'. Catching the recon-to-persistence chain on a single host within 30 minutes — by the same actor account — is far higher fidelity than alerting on either side alone, and matches the playbook documented in the Bitdefender/ESET intrusion timelines.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as recon_time values(Processes.process) as recon_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND (Processes.process="*process list*" OR Processes.process="*process get*" OR Processes.process="*/node:*" OR Processes.process="*antivirus*" OR Processes.process="*computersystem*" OR Processes.process="*useraccount*" OR Processes.process="*ntdomain*" OR Processes.process="*nicconfig*") by host Processes.user
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| rename user as recon_user
| join type=inner host [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as persist_time values(Processes.process) as persist_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="schtasks.exe" AND Processes.process="*/create*") OR (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND Processes.process="*process*" AND Processes.process="*call*" AND Processes.process="*create*") by host Processes.user
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
    | rename user as persist_user
]
| eval delta_sec=persist_time-recon_time
| where delta_sec>=0 AND delta_sec<=1800 AND recon_user=persist_user AND NOT match(recon_user,"\\$$")
| table recon_time persist_time delta_sec host recon_user recon_cmd persist_cmd
| sort - recon_time
ucs.kql:
// Naikon recon → persistence chain: WMIC discovery cluster followed by
// schtasks /create or 'wmic process call create' on the same host
// by the same account within 30 minutes.
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let ChainWindow = 30m;
let _wmic_recon_tokens = dynamic([
    "process list","process get","/node:","antivirus","computersystem",
    "useraccount","ntdomain","nicconfig","product get","service get","os get"
]);
let WmicRecon =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where FileName =~ "wmic.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (_wmic_recon_tokens)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"                                 // skip machine accounts
    | summarize ReconStart = min(Timestamp),
                ReconCmds = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 25),
                ReconCount = count()
        by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName
    | where ReconCount >= 2;                                          // require a CLUSTER, not a one-off
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where (FileName =~ "schtasks.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/create")
     or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("process","call","create"))
| project PersistTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
          PersistBin = FileName,
          PersistCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          PersistParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          PersistParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| join kind=inner WmicRecon on DeviceId, AccountName
| where PersistTime between (ReconStart .. ReconStart + ChainWindow)
| extend DeltaSec = datetime_diff('second', PersistTime, ReconStart)
| project ReconStart, PersistTime, DeltaSec, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ReconCount, ReconCmds, PersistBin, PersistCmd, PersistParent, PersistParentCmd
| order by ReconStart desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sentinel via 4688 (requires 'Include command line in process creation events' GPO).
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let ChainWindow = 30m;
let _wmic_recon_tokens = dynamic([
    "process list","process get","/node:","antivirus","computersystem",
    "useraccount","ntdomain","nicconfig","product get","service get","os get"
]);
let WmicRecon =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith @"\wmic.exe"
    | where CommandLine has_any (_wmic_recon_tokens)
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | summarize ReconStart = min(TimeGenerated),
                ReconCmds = make_set(CommandLine, 25),
                ReconCount = count()
        by Computer, Account
    | where ReconCount >= 2;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where (NewProcessName endswith @"\schtasks.exe" and CommandLine has "/create")
     or (NewProcessName endswith @"\wmic.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("process","call","create"))
| project PersistTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          PersistBin = NewProcessName, PersistCmd = CommandLine,
          PersistParent = ParentProcessName
| join kind=inner WmicRecon on Computer, Account
| where PersistTime between (ReconStart .. ReconStart + ChainWindow)
| extend DeltaSec = datetime_diff('second', PersistTime, ReconStart)
| project ReconStart, PersistTime, DeltaSec, Computer, Account,
          ReconCount, ReconCmds, PersistBin, PersistCmd, PersistParent
| order by ReconStart desc
ucs.rationale: Each individual leg (WMIC recon, schtasks /create, 'wmic process call create') is too noisy for an alert in a real estate — sysadmins, SCCM, and patching tools generate hundreds per day. Naikon is one of the very few actors who routinely chains a *cluster* of WMIC discovery commands (especially the AV/computersystem/ntdomain combo) directly into persistence via schtasks or remote WMI process creat
